title: stigmatodon croceanus
intro_study: According to "BMC complementary and alternative medicine", Stigmatodon croceanus (also known as Crocus sativus L. or saffron) may be of therapeutic benefit in the treatment of mild to moderate depression.
side_effects: Stigmatodon croceanus can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, which can manifest as skin irritation, itching, and swelling. Ingestion of the plant can cause gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain. Overexposure to the plant's toxins can also lead to respiratory problems, such as coughing, wheezing, and shortness of breath. Improper use of Stigmatodon croceanus can also cause neurological symptoms, including headaches, dizziness, and in severe cases, seizures.
